title: HTML profile page injects unescaped domain into page title and body
description: In server.rs serve_profile_html() and index(), state.domain is interpolated directly into the HTML template with format\!() — it is never passed through ammonia::clean() or HTML-escaped. The domain value comes from config.toml. If an operator sets domain to something containing '<', '>', '&', or '"' characters (e.g., a misconfiguration or a deliberately crafted value), the HTML output will be malformed. While the operator controls the config, this is a footgun: config values are not validated for HTML-safety on load, and anyone who copies a domain string with an ambiguous character will silently produce broken or injectable pages.
close_reason: Fixed: domain HTML-escaped with ammonia::clean before interpolation.
